summary: pulumi_vcd typosquat redirects Pulumi provider plugin download to a personal GitHub user
details: The package name mimics the official Pulumi-namespaced VCD provider, but its Pulumi plugin metadata redirects the provider binary download to a personal GitHub account rather than the `pulumi` organization. `pulumi_vcd/_utilities.py` defines `get_plugin_download_url()` returning `github://api.github.com/ergSey/pulumi-vcd`, and `pulumi_vcd/pulumi-plugin.json` declares the same `server` URL. When a developer installs this SDK and runs Pulumi, the engine fetches and executes the provider plugin binary from that personal repository with no hash or signature pinning. The provider runs locally with the user's vCloud credentials (api_token, password) in scope, so any binary published at that release URL achieves code execution on the installer's machine with cloud credentials available. Supporting indicators: placeholder README pointing VCD to `http://example.com` and a 10-digit epoch-style alpha version (`3.0.0a1779710724`) consistent with automated squat tooling, not a maintained provider.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
